message:
I am thinking...: The template file doesn't exist in the filesystem. I need to use the template format as described in my workflow instructions. The workflow says to use the template at `nc_agents/agents/mcu_factory_verification_planning/templates/scenario_template.yaml`, but this file doesn't exist. I'll use the schema described in my workflow instructions which includes:

1. The scenario YAML schema with:
   - name
   - description
   - checklist_ids
   - test_name
   - setup
   - external_devices (with required `type` field)
   - stimulus
   - expected_behavior / checks
   - pass_criteria

2. The external_devices schema from the workflow:
   - name
   - type (uart, i2c_memory, spi_slave, gpio_stimulus)
   - connect
   - params

Now let me design the full checklist. Based on the requirements and spec documents I've read, here are the peripherals and behaviors to cover:

## Peripheral Inventory
1. CPU/Core - hazard3 RV32I
2. XIP Flash Controller - 512KB, NC_XIP
3. SRAM - 128KB (4×32KB banks)
4. GPIO - 3 instances (GPIOA, GPIOB, GPIOC), 16 pins each
5. UART - 4 instances (UART0-UART3)
6. SPI - 2 instances (SPI0-SPI1)  
7. I2C - 3 instances (I2C0-I2C2, where I2C2 is the new derivative addition)
8. Timers - 6 instances (TMR0-TMR5)
9. PWM - 2 instances (PWM0-PWM1)
10. DMA Controller - 1 instance, 8 channels
11. CRC Accelerator
12. AES Accelerator (AES-128/256)
13. WWDT (Windowed Watchdog)
14. RTC (Real-Time Clock with calendar)
15. SysTick
16. Bus fabric (AHB-Lite + APB)
17. Interrupt controller (Hazard3 PLIC)
18. Reset architecture
19. Clock architecture
20. Debug (SWD)

## Checklist Structure
I'll organize by domain:
- SYS: System-level (reset, clock, boot)
- XIP: Execute-in-place flash
- MEM: SRAM memory
- GPIO: GPIO port tests
- UART: UART tests  
- SPI: SPI tests
- I2C: I2C tests
- TMR: Timer tests
- PWM: PWM tests
- DMA: DMA controller tests
- CRC: CRC accelerator tests
- AES: AES accelerator tests
- WWDT: Watchdog tests
- RTC: Real-time clock tests
- SYSTICK: SysTick tests
- IRQ: Interrupt controller tests
- DBG: Debug/SWD tests
- INT: Integration tests

Let me define all items:

### SYS (System)
- SYS-01: Power-on reset sequence - all peripherals in reset state
- SYS-02: External nRESET assertion de-asserts core
- SYS-03: Software reset (SYSRESETREQ) resets CPU and peripherals
- SYS-04: Clock starts at correct frequency after reset release
- SYS-05: WWDT timeout causes full chip reset

### XIP (Execute-in-Place Flash)
- XIP-01: Boot from XIP flash at address 0x00000000
- XIP-02: XIP register smoke test (read/write CSRs)
- XIP-03: XIP read data integrity (known pattern verification)
- XIP-04: XIP address boundary check (512KB limit)

### MEM (SRAM)
- MEM-01: SRAM write/read at base address 0x20000000
- MEM-02: SRAM full 128KB address range walking test
- MEM-03: SRAM read-after-write data integrity
- MEM-04: SRAM simultaneous access (CPU + DMA)

### GPIO (GPIO Ports)
- GPIO-01: GPIOA output direction control and data output
- GPIO-02: GPIOA input read-back
- GPIO-03: GPIOB output direction control and data output
- GPIO-04: GPIOB input read-back
- GPIO-05: GPIOC output direction control and data output
- GPIO-06: GPIOC input read-back
- GPIO-07: GPIO alternate function selection (pinmux)
- GPIO-08: GPIO interrupt on rising/falling edge
- GPIO-09: GPIO reset state (all inputs Hi-Z)

### UART
- UART-01: UART0 TX/RX loopback at 115200 baud
- UART-02: UART1 TX/RX loopback at 115200 baud
- UART-03: UART2 TX/RX loopback at 115200 baud
- UART-04: UART3 TX/RX loopback at 115200 baud
- UART-05: UART0 FIFO fill and drain (8-deep TX/RX FIFO)
- UART-06: UART0 framing error detection
- UART-07: UART0 baud rate configuration (multiple rates)
- UART-08: UART register smoke test (all instances)

### SPI
- SPI-01: SPI0 master-mode transfer (CPOL=0, CPHA=0)
- SPI-02: SPI0 master-mode transfer (CPOL=1, CPHA=1)
- SPI-03: SPI1 master-mode transfer
- SPI-04: SPI0 8-bit and 16-bit frame modes
- SPI-05: SPI register smoke test (all instances)

### I2C
- I2C-01: I2C0 7-bit address write/read transaction
- I2C-02: I2C1 7-bit address write/read transaction
- I2C-03: I2C2 7-bit address write/read transaction (derivative new instance)
- I2C-04: I2C0 10-bit address mode
- I2C-05: I2C0 fast-mode (400kHz) transaction
- I2C-06: I2C0 fast-plus-mode (1MHz) transaction
- I2C-07: I2C register smoke test (all instances including I2C2)

### TMR (Timers)
- TMR-01: TMR0 overflow interrupt (GP timer)
- TMR-02: TMR1 capture mode
- TMR-03: TMR2 compare match interrupt
- TMR-04: TMR3 prescaler configuration
- TMR-05: TMR4 advanced control features (up/down count)
- TMR-06: TMR5 advanced control features
- TMR-07: Timer register smoke test (all 6 instances)

### PWM
- PWM-01: PWM0 period and duty cycle configuration
- PWM-02: PWM1 complementary output with dead-time
- PWM-03: PWM fault input response
- PWM-04: PWM register smoke test

### DMA
- DMA-01: DMA memory-to-memory transfer (single channel)
- DMA-02: DMA peripheral-to-memory transfer (UART RX)
- DMA-03: DMA memory-to-peripheral transfer (UART TX)
- DMA-04: DMA 8-channel concurrent operation
- DMA-05: DMA transfer complete interrupt
- DMA-06: DMA error interrupt
- DMA-07: DMA channel priority arbitration

### CRC
- CRC-01: CRC-32 known-answer test
- CRC-02: CRC-16 known-answer test
- CRC-03: CRC-8 known-answer test
- CRC-04: CRC register smoke test

### AES
- AES-01: AES-128 ECB encryption known-answer test
- AES-02: AES-256 ECB encryption known-answer test
- AES-03: AES-128 CBC mode encryption/decryption
- AES-04: AES DMA-assisted bulk encryption
- AES-05: AES done interrupt

### WWDT
- WWDT-01: WWDT normal service within window (no reset)
- WWDT-02: WWDT early-warning interrupt before window opens
- WWDT-03: WWDT timeout reset (service outside window)
- WWDT-04: WWDT window constraint violation triggers reset

### RTC
- RTC-01: RTC calendar set and read-back
- RTC-02: RTC Alarm A fires at configured time
- RTC-03: RTC Alarm B fires at configured time
- RTC-04: RTC periodic wakeup timer interrupt
- RTC-05: RTC time advancement over seconds/minutes/hours

### SYSTICK
- SYSTICK-01: SysTick underflow interrupt
- SYSTICK-02: SysTick reload value and count
- SYSTICK-03: SysTick enable/disable

### IRQ
- IRQ-01: All peripheral IRQs assertion and acknowledgment (each peripheral's IRQ vector fires)
- IRQ-02: IRQ priority arbitration
- IRQ-03: IRQ nesting and re-entrancy
- IRQ-04: GPIO edge interrupt (IRQ0-2 GPIOA/B/C)

### DBG (Debug)
- DBG-01: SWD DAP connection and register access
- DBG-02: SWD halt and resume CPU execution
- DBG-03: SWD memory read/write via DAP

### INT (Integration)
- INT-01: Full boot from XIP flash - CPU fetches and executes code
- INT-02: Multi-peripheral concurrent operation (UART + SPI + I2C simultaneously)
- INT-03: DMA-assisted UART transmission (end-to-end)
- INT-04: AES encryption of data then CRC check of ciphertext
- INT-05: GPIO-triggered DMA transfer

That gives us a rich checklist. Now let me organize by scenario groups to minimize the number of YAML files while maintaining logical grouping.

Scenario files to generate:
1. scenario_sys_reset_clock.yaml - SYS-01 through SYS-05
2. scenario_xip_flash.yaml - XIP-01 through XIP-04
3. scenario_sram_memory.yaml - MEM-01 through MEM-04
4. scenario_gpio_basic.yaml - GPIO-01 through GPIO-06, GPIO-09
5. scenario_gpio_af_irq.yaml - GPIO-07, GPIO-08, IRQ-04
6. scenario_uart_loopback.yaml - UART-01 through UART-04
7. scenario_uart_advanced.yaml - UART-05 through UART-08
8. scenario_spi_transfer.yaml - SPI-01 through SPI-05
9. scenario_i2c_transfer.yaml - I2C-01 through I2C-07
10. scenario_timer_basic.yaml - TMR-01 through TMR-07
11. scenario_pwm_output.yaml - PWM-01 through PWM-04
12. scenario_dma_transfer.yaml - DMA-01 through DMA-07
13. scenario_crc_kat.yaml - CRC-01 through CRC-04
14. scenario_aes_kat.yaml - AES-01 through AES-05
15. scenario_wwdt_window.yaml - WWDT-01 through WWDT-04
16. scenario_rtc_calendar.yaml - RTC-01 through RTC-05
17. scenario_systick_timer.yaml - SYSTICK-01 through SYSTICK-03
18. scenario_irq_fabric.yaml - IRQ-01 through IRQ-03
19. scenario_swd_debug.yaml - DBG-01 through DBG-03
20. scenario_integration_boot.yaml - INT-01
21. scenario_integration_multiperipheral.yaml - INT-02 through INT-05

That's 21 scenario files.

Now I need to ensure each checklist item lists a scenario name, and the manifest maps everything correctly.

Let me also make sure to use the correct ID regex `[A-Z0-9]+-\d+` since I2C has digits in the prefix.

- IRQ-01: All peripheral IRQs assertion and acknowledgment (each peripheral's IRQ vector fires)
- IRQ-02: IRQ priority arbitration
- IRQ-03: IRQ nesting and re-entrancy
- IRQ-04: GPIO edge interrupt (IRQ0-2 GPIOA/B/C)

### DBG (Debug)
- DBG-01: SWD DAP connection and register access
- DBG-02: SWD halt and resume CPU execution
- DBG-03: SWD memory read/write via DAP

### INT (Integration)
- INT-01: Full boot from XIP flash - CPU fetches and executes code
- INT-02: Multi-peripheral concurrent operation (UART + SPI + I2C simultaneously)
- INT-03: DMA-assisted UART transmission (end-to-end)
- INT-04: AES encryption of data then CRC check of ciphertext
- INT-05: GPIO-triggered DMA transfer

That gives us a rich checklist. Now let me organize by scenario groups to minimize the number of YAML files while maintaining logical grouping.

Scenario files to generate:
1. scenario_sys_reset_clock.yaml - SYS-01 through SYS-05
2. scenario_xip_flash.yaml - XIP-01 through XIP-04
3. scenario_sram_memory.yaml - MEM-01 through MEM-04
4. scenario_gpio_basic.yaml - GPIO-01 through GPIO-06, GPIO-09
5. scenario_gpio_af_irq.yaml - GPIO-07, GPIO-08, IRQ-04
6. scenario_uart_loopback.yaml - UART-01 through UART-04
7. scenario_uart_advanced.yaml - UART-05 through UART-08
8. scenario_spi_transfer.yaml - SPI-01 through SPI-05
9. scenario_i2c_transfer.yaml - I2C-01 through I2C-07
10. scenario_timer_basic.yaml - TMR-01 through TMR-07
11. scenario_pwm_output.yaml - PWM-01 through PWM-04
12. scenario_dma_transfer.yaml - DMA-01 through DMA-07
13. scenario_crc_kat.yaml - CRC-01 through CRC-04
14. scenario_aes_kat.yaml - AES-01 through AES-05
15. scenario_wwdt_window.yaml - WWDT-01 through WWDT-04
16. scenario_rtc_calendar.yaml - RTC-01 through RTC-05
17. scenario_systick_timer.yaml - SYSTICK-01 through SYSTICK-03
18. scenario_irq_fabric.yaml - IRQ-01 through IRQ-03
19. scenario_swd_debug.yaml - DBG-01 through DBG-03
20. scenario_integration_boot.yaml - INT-01
21. scenario_integration_multiperipheral.yaml - INT-02 through INT-05

That's 21 scenario files.

Now I need to ensure each checklist item lists a scenario name, and the manifest maps everything correctly.

Let me also make sure to use the correct ID regex `[A-Z0-9]+-\d+` since I2C has digits in the prefix.
